meta._comment: SNAPSHOT of NOAA SWPC space-weather data. Space weather is a genuine operational forecasting domain (SWPC issues real forecasts); this project VISUALIZES SWPC's measurements and forecasts and never invents its own. All SWPC feeds are U.S. Gov public domain and CORS-enabled (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *), so the app fetches them LIVE client-side; this committed file is the offline / first-paint / rate-limit fallback.
meta.license: U.S. Government work, public domain (17 U.S.C. 105)

COMPUTED: aurora activity level + oval latitude from Kp

solar_wind.instrument: DSCOVR (primary) / ACE, propagated to Earth's bow shock
solar_wind.note: Real-time solar wind measured at L1 and propagated to the bow shock. Southward Bz (negative) couples energy into the magnetosphere and drives aurora.

planetary_k_index.note: Kp is a measured global geomagnetic-activity index. The 3-day-ahead Kp FORECAST is a separate SWPC product, attributed to SWPC when shown.

aurora.attribution: NOAA SWPC OVATION Prime aurora model (SWPC's forecast)

kp_derived_activity._oval_note: COMPUTED coarse approximation lat=66.5-2.0*Kp; rule-of-thumb only, not a forecast

aurora.note: The OVATION aurora nowcast is SWPC's OWN model output (instantaneous probability of visible aurora per grid cell). We VISUALIZE it and attribute it to SWPC; we do not forecast aurora ourselves. The full ~65k-point grid is fetched LIVE by the app; only a summary is committed here. kp_derived_activity is COMPUTED from the measured Kp, not from OVATION.

xray.instrument: GOES-primary X-Ray Sensor (XRS), 0.1-0.8 nm long channel
xray.note: Solar soft X-ray flux; flare class (A/B/C/M/X) is a base-10 scale on the 0.1-0.8 nm flux. M/X flares can trigger radio blackouts. Real GOES measurement.

sunspot_number.value_swpc_source: NOAA SWPC monthly sunspot number (observed_swpc_ssn) -- U.S. Gov public domain
sunspot_number.value_isn_source: International Sunspot Number v2.0, WDC-SILSO, Royal Observatory of Belgium (CC BY-NC 4.0), taken here from NOAA's public-domain redistribution
sunspot_number.note: Two sunspot counts exist: NOAA's own (public domain, used as primary) and the international ISN from WDC-SILSO (CC BY-NC). We display the public-domain NOAA number.

f10_7.unit: solar flux units (10^-22 W m^-2 Hz^-1) at 10.7 cm / 2800 MHz
f10_7.note: F10.7 (Penticton 10.7 cm radio flux) is a standard proxy for solar activity / EUV output. Monthly value shown.

solar_cycle.phase: Declining phase -- near / just after the Cycle-25 maximum (~late 2024). Smoothed sunspot number has passed its peak and is trending down.
solar_cycle.note: Cycle-25 amplitude (smoothed SSN peak ~160) ran notably higher than the official 2019 SWPC/NOAA-NASA panel consensus prediction (~115). Peak month is a best estimate: the 13-month smoothing lags real time by ~6 months, so the most recent months are not yet smoothable.
